title: hearth.liaison.reply v1
description: Emitted by hearth-api's routes/liaison.rs (POST /api/liaison/reply/{insight_id}) when Eric sends a 'rubber-duck' free-text reply to a career-ops proposition card in the Liaison phone feed — an idea, objection, question, connection, or todo that should feed back into the machinery rather than a structured answer/dismiss action. The reply is first persisted to hearth-api's liaison_replies table, then mirrored here. Consumers: career-ops sessions (to fold the reply into the next relevant working session), kb (durable capture). SENSITIVE-DATA NOTE: this event lands in the shared nervous-bus debug log (~/.cache/nervous-bus/debug.jsonl — world-tailed into Redis for any consumer). Publishers MUST NOT put verbatim personal/financial detail in `reply`; prefer a short category-level label plus enough context for a consumer to go find the durable record (liaison_replies row via `reply_id`) rather than inlining sensitive specifics here.

reply_id.description: UUID primary key of the liaison_replies row this event mirrors.

insight_id.description: CognitiveBus Insight id the reply was attached to (the proposition card being rubber-ducked).

source_refs.description: The proposition's source_ids (e.g. demand-signal ids, bet slugs, steering-queue ids) the reply refers back to, for traceability.

reply.description: Eric's rubber-duck reply text (NOT verbatim sensitive detail — see schema-level SENSITIVE-DATA NOTE; prefer category + short text).

category.description: What kind of reply this is, for routing/triage by consumers.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp the reply was submitted.
